title: Audiologists
description: Assess and treat persons with hearing and related disorders. May fit hearing aids and provide auditory training. May perform research related to hearing problems.

tasks.text: Maintain patient records at all stages, including initial and subsequent evaluation and treatment activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate hearing and balance disorders to determine diagnoses and courses of treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fit, dispense, and repair assistive devices, such as hearing aids.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Administer hearing tests and examine patients to collect information on type and degree of impairment, using specialized instruments and electronic equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Monitor patients' progress and provide ongoing observation of hearing or balance status.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Instruct patients, parents, teachers, or employers in communication strategies to maximize effective receptive communication.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Counsel and instruct patients and their families in techniques to improve hearing and communication related to hearing loss.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Refer patients to additional medical or educational services, if needed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Participate in conferences or training to update or share knowledge of new hearing or balance disorder treatment methods or technologies.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine and clean patients' ear canals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend assistive devices according to patients' needs or nature of impairments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise educators or other medical staff on hearing or balance topics.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Program and monitor cochlear implants to fit the needs of patients.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate and supervise audiology students and health care personnel.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan and conduct treatment programs for patients' hearing or balance problems, consulting with educators, physicians, nurses, psychologists, speech-language pathologists, and other health care personnel, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Work with multidisciplinary teams to assess and rehabilitate recipients of implanted hearing devices through auditory training and counseling.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct or direct research on hearing or balance topics and report findings to help in the development of procedures, technology, or treatments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform administrative tasks, such as managing office functions and finances.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide information to the public on hearing or balance topics.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Engage in marketing activities, such as developing marketing plans, to promote business for private practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Measure noise levels in workplaces and conduct hearing conservation programs in industry, military, schools, and communities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and supervise hearing screening programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
